question: International T20 Series: Netherlands vs Zimbabwe (Game 1) - Most Sixes Netherlands Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Netherlands and Zimbabwe scheduled for 2026-02-03 in World Cup Warm-up Matches.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Netherlands will be considered correct if Netherlands is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Zimbabwe.The outcome corresponding to Zimbabwe will be considered correct if Zimbabwe is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Netherlands.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

description:

This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Netherlands will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from Netherlands.

question: International T20 Series: Nepal vs UAE (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Nepal and UAE scheduled for February 3 2026 in International T20 Series.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: International T20 Series: Nepal vs UAE (Game 1) - Most Sixes Nepal Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Nepal and UAE scheduled for 2026-02-03 in World Cup Warm-up Matches.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Nepal will be considered correct if Nepal is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than UAE.The outcome corresponding to UAE will be considered correct if UAE is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Nepal.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: International T20 Series: Nepal vs UAE (Game 1) - Team Top Batter Nepal Winner
description:
This market refers to which team's player records the highest individual run total in the cricket match between Nepal and UAE scheduled for 2026-02-03 in World Cup Warm-up Matches.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Nepal will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from Nepal.
The outcome corresponding to UAE will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from UAE.
If the highest individual score is shared by at least one player from each team, the market will resolve to Draw.
The market will resolve according to which team's player records the highest individual run total regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions).
DLS/DRS adjustments and any other on-field rulings affecting batting statistics are treated as ordinary data.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official batting statistics being recorded, the market will resolve Draw.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: International T20 Series: Namibia vs Scotland (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Namibia and Scotland scheduled for February 4 2026 in International T20 Series.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: International T20 Series: Namibia vs Scotland (Game 1) - Who wins the toss?
description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Namibia and Scotland scheduled for 2026-02-04 in World Cup Warm-up Matches.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: International T20 Series: Namibia vs Scotland (Game 1)
events.description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Namibia and Scotland scheduled for February 4 2026 in International T20 Series.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: International T20 Series: Namibia vs Scotland (Game 1) - Completed match?
description:
This market refers to whether the cricket match between Namibia and Scotland scheduled for 2026-02-04 in World Cup Warm-up Matches is completed under the competition's official playing conditions.
This market resolves according to the finalized match status as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
Any match where an official result is declared — including results decided by DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, a Super Over, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling — will resolve Yes. (Draws count as completed results when recognized by the competition.)
If the match is abandoned, ends in No Result, is permanently canceled, or otherwise concludes without an official result, the market will resolve No.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed or permanently canceled, after which it will resolve as described above.

question: International T20 Series: Namibia vs Scotland (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Namibia Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Namibia and Scotland scheduled for 2026-02-04 in World Cup Warm-up Matches.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Namibia will be considered correct if  Namibia is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Scotland will be considered correct if Scotland is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: International T20 Series: Ireland vs Pakistan (Game 1)
description:

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.

question: International T20 Series: Ireland vs Pakistan (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Ireland Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Ireland and Pakistan scheduled for 2026-02-04 in World Cup Warm-up Matches.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Ireland will be considered correct if  Ireland is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Pakistan will be considered correct if Pakistan is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: International T20 Series: Ireland vs Pakistan (Game 1) - Most Sixes Ireland Winner
description:

question: International T20 Series: Canada vs Nepal (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Canada and Nepal scheduled for February 5 2026 in International T20 Series.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: International T20 Series: Canada vs Nepal (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Canada Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Canada and Nepal scheduled for 2026-02-05 in World Cup Warm-up Matches.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Canada will be considered correct if  Canada is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Nepal will be considered correct if Nepal is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: International T20 Series: Australia vs Netherlands (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Australia and Netherlands scheduled for February 5 2026 in International T20 Series.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: International T20 Series: Australia vs Netherlands (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Australia Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Australia and Netherlands scheduled for 2026-02-05 in World Cup Warm-up Matches.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Australia will be considered correct if  Australia is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Netherlands will be considered correct if Netherlands is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

This market refers to which team's player records the highest individual run total in the cricket match between Australia and Netherlands scheduled for 2026-02-05 in World Cup Warm-up Matches.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Australia will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from Australia.
The outcome corresponding to Netherlands will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from Netherlands.
If the highest individual score is shared by at least one player from each team, the market will resolve to Draw.
The market will resolve according to which team's player records the highest individual run total regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions).
DLS/DRS adjustments and any other on-field rulings affecting batting statistics are treated as ordinary data.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official batting statistics being recorded, the market will resolve Draw.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: International T20 Series: New Zealand vs USA (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between New Zealand and USA scheduled for February 5 2026 in International T20 Series.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: International T20 Series: New Zealand vs USA (Game 1) - Who wins the toss?
description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between New Zealand and USA scheduled for 2026-02-05 in World Cup Warm-up Matches.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

question: International T20 Series: New Zealand vs USA (Game 1) - Completed match?
description:
This market refers to whether the cricket match between New Zealand and USA scheduled for 2026-02-05 in World Cup Warm-up Matches is completed under the competition's official playing conditions.
This market resolves according to the finalized match status as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
Any match where an official result is declared — including results decided by DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, a Super Over, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling — will resolve Yes. (Draws count as completed results when recognized by the competition.)
If the match is abandoned, ends in No Result, is permanently canceled, or otherwise concludes without an official result, the market will resolve No.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed or permanently canceled, after which it will resolve as described above.

question: International T20 Series: New Zealand vs USA (Game 1) - Toss Match Double New Zealand Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between New Zealand and USA scheduled for 2026-02-05 in World Cup Warm-up Matches.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to New Zealand will be considered correct if  New Zealand is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to USA will be considered correct if USA is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: International T20 Series: New Zealand vs USA (Game 1) - Most Sixes New Zealand Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between New Zealand and USA scheduled for 2026-02-05 in World Cup Warm-up Matches.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to New Zealand will be considered correct if New Zealand is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than USA.The outcome corresponding to USA will be considered correct if USA is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than New Zealand.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

description:

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.

question: International T20 Series: Italy vs UAE (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Italy Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Italy and UAE scheduled for 2026-02-06 in World Cup Warm-up Matches.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Italy will be considered correct if  Italy is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to UAE will be considered correct if UAE is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: International T20 Series: Italy vs UAE (Game 1) - Most Sixes Italy Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Italy and UAE scheduled for 2026-02-06 in World Cup Warm-up Matches.

The outcome corresponding to Italy will be considered correct if Italy is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than UAE.The outcome corresponding to UAE will be considered correct if UAE is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Italy.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".

question: International T20 Series: Afghanistan vs West Indies (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Afghanistan and West Indies scheduled for February 4 2026 in International T20 Series.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: International T20 Series: Afghanistan vs West Indies (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Afghanistan Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Afghanistan and West Indies scheduled for 2026-02-04 in World Cup Warm-up Matches.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.

If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: International T20 Series: Afghanistan vs West Indies (Game 1) - Most Sixes Afghanistan Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Afghanistan and West Indies scheduled for 2026-02-04 in World Cup Warm-up Matches.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Afghanistan will be considered correct if Afghanistan is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than West Indies.The outcome corresponding to West Indies will be considered correct if West Indies is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Afghanistan.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: International T20 Series: India vs South Africa (Game 1) - Most Sixes India Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between India and South Africa scheduled for 2026-02-04 in World Cup Warm-up Matches.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to India will be considered correct if India is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than South Africa.The outcome corresponding to South Africa will be considered correct if South Africa is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than India.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: International T20 Series: Pakistan vs Australia (Game 2) - Most Sixes Pakistan Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Pakistan and Australia scheduled for 2026-01-31 in Pakistan T20 Series.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Pakistan will be considered correct if Pakistan is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Australia.The outcome corresponding to Australia will be considered correct if Australia is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Pakistan.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: International T20 Series: Pakistan vs Australia (Game 3) - Most Sixes Pakistan Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Pakistan and Australia scheduled for 2026-02-01 in Pakistan T20 Series.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Pakistan will be considered correct if Pakistan is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Australia.The outcome corresponding to Australia will be considered correct if Australia is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Pakistan.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.
